articles.title: Dollar Recovers on Conflicting US-Iran Peace Plans
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) on Wednesday rose by +0.03%. The dollar recovered from early losses on Wednesday and posted modest gains on conflicting signals about prospects for a US-Iran deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. articles.title: Financial & Forex Market Recap – May 27, 2026
articles.expand: Equities held near record highs Wednesday while Iran deal uncertainty sent oil sharply lower and a hawkish RBNZ surprise drove the New Zealand dollar to the session’s top spot.

articles.title: Aussie cracks as cool CPI meets a stubborn Kiwi
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar finally tripped on Wednesday, dropping nearly 2% against the New Zealand Dollar. AUD/NZD had been grinding to generational highs near 1.2300, fuelled by a story of relative central bank divergence that always looked a little overcooked.

articles.title: Fed Cook: Warns rate hike possible as inflation worsens
articles.expand: Fed Governor Lisa Cook crossed the wires, saying the right course of action is to keep rates steady due to upside risks to inflation, which is moving in the “wrong direction.”

articles.title: Crude Oil prices a deal the White House calls a lie
articles.expand: Iran's state broadcaster said Wednesday that Tehran had received an initial draft of a 14-point Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) framework with the United States, one that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz, lift the US naval blockade on Iranian ports, and pull US forces back from Iranian territory.

articles.title: Solar investment surges as Ghana races to hit renewable targets
articles.expand: Ghana's renewable energy capacity stands at around 280 megawatts, roughly 5 percent of the national energy mix. The government wants 7 percent by 2027. Getting there will require all the resources the country can mobilise: legislation, public investment and private capital.
